STAY CONNECTED

Martín Llaryora 
Governor of the Province of Córdoba, Argentina 
In today's global environment, competitiveness is increasingly determined by our ability to innovate, 
collaborate, and develop solutions that integrate knowledge with productive activities. Regions, provinces, and 
cities have emerged as pivotal players in this process, driving economic growth and catalyzing tangible 
responses to global challenges. 
In Córdoba, we are committed to this responsibility, with a clear vision: to build a more competitive, 
innovative, and globally integrated province through coordination between the public, private, academic, and 
scientific sectors. 
It is with great pleasure that we extend a cordial welcome to you for the 22nd Edition of the Americas 
Competitiveness Exchange (ACE), a prestigious event organized by the Government of the Province of Córdoba 
in collaboration with the Organization of American States (OAS), with the support of the United States 
Government. 
ACE Córdoba 2025 will bring together leaders from government, business, academia, and international 
organizations from across the continent to share experiences, explore opportunities for cooperation and 
investment, and strengthen the networks that drive competitiveness in the Americas. 
Throughout the week, the international delegation will visit projects and companies representing the most 
dynamic sectors in our province. These sectors include agribusiness and AgTech; the automotive and auto parts 
industry; connectivity and digital transformation; startup and innovation ecosystems; and HealthTech, Biotech, 
and life sciences. 
This high-level meeting will provide a first-hand look at a province with a diversified economy, an expanding 
innovation ecosystem, and a collaborative governance model that enhances territorial competitiveness. 
The "Córdoba Model" — a collaborative effort between government, businesses, and universities — has been 
instrumental in promoting investment, driving productive transformation, and generating quality employment. 
Córdoba's competitiveness is demonstrated by its engagement in various sectors, including agribusiness, 
metalworking, knowledge-based products and services, the circular economy, and tourism. This 
competitiveness is built on multi-stakeholder and multi-level governance, integrating visions, capabilities, and 
efforts toward a common development goal. 
We are pleased to welcome you to our province, confident that this edition of the ACE will strengthen 
cooperation networks, open new opportunities for business and innovation, and consolidate the Americas as a 
more prosperous, sustainable, and connected region. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Martín Llaryora 
Governor of the Province of Córdoba 
Argentine Republica

WELCOME FROM 
 THE ACE COMMITTEE
Dear distinguished leaders from the Americas and beyond,
It is our great honor to welcome you to the 22nd edition of the Americas 
Competitiveness Exchange (ACE) – Córdoba 2025. This program is a dynamic 
platform fostering cooperation, innovation, and the creation of strategic 
partnerships to drive economic development across the Americas.
ACE Córdoba is convened by the Organization of American States (OAS) and 
the Government of the Province of Córdoba, Argentina, with the support of 
the United States Government, through the Department of State – Permanent 
Mission to the OAS, and the Department of Commerce – Economic Development 
Administration.
From November 2 to 7, 2025, you will experience firsthand Córdoba’s innovative 
economic landscape, engaging with leading actors in Agribusiness and 
AgTech; Life Sciences, HealthTech, and Biotechnology; Connectivity and Digital 
Transformation; and the Automotive and Auto Parts Industry. The agenda will 
take you across several dynamic cities—Córdoba, General Cabrera, Río Cuarto, 
General Deheza, Arroyito, and San Francisco—where you will meet inspiring 
leaders from government, academia, and the private sector.
The partnerships you build in ACE Córdoba can pave the way for future 
collaborations in trade, investment, research and development, entrepreneurship, 
and beyond. To support your engagement, this Tripbook provides essential 
information about the agenda, participants, and key contacts, serving as your 
guide to explore opportunities and deepen cooperation.
The ACE Committee extends its deepest appreciation to the Government of the 
Province of Córdoba and all partners involved for designing an exceptional program 
that reflects the region’s innovative spirit and commitment to economic growth.
We look forward to an inspiring and productive week together in Córdoba.
Sincerely,
The ACE Committee
Americas
Competitiveness
Exchange

5
 CÓRDOBA
OVERVIEW
WHAT SETS CÓRDOBA APART?
 True productive diversification: Córdoba has a diversified economic 
structure where agribusiness, automotive, agricultural machinery, tourism, 
and the knowledge economy coexist.
 An academic ecosystem of continental scale: With the National University 
of Córdoba (400 years old, ranked among the top 50 in Latin America) 
and 11 other universities, the Province combines academic tradition with 
applied innovation, turning research into startups and productive solutions.
 Fiscal and political stability: Córdoba has maintained a fiscal surplus since 
2020, offering a predictable environment for business and investment.
 Public–private–academic collaboration model: This triple helix has proven 
effective in designing consensus-based public policies, driving innovation, 
and strengthening territorial competitiveness.
 Strategic infrastructure for regional integration: Its central location, 
combined with the new dry port and connectivity infrastructure, 
distinguishes Córdoba as a strategic point for productive and commercial 
integration in South America.
 Culture of innovation with strong local roots: From more than 27 
productive clusters to global startups born in Córdoba, the Province blends 
its industrial and agricultural tradition with an innovation ecosystem that 
continues to expand into the future.
 Quality of life and cultural appeal: Córdoba offers a rich environment 
of cultural and historic patrimony, natural beauty, and high quality of 
life, enhancing its attractiveness as a place to live, invest, and collaborate 
long-term.

6
 ECONOMIC 
SECTORS
Agribusiness
& AgriTech
Automotive & 
Auto Parts Industry
Córdoba is Argentina’s agribusiness crown jewel, creating 
more than 78,000 jobs and contributing nearly 30% 
of the country’s agricultural GDP. Main crops include 
corn, soybeans, peanuts, and 
sorghum. Córdoba 
generates 70% of the national peanut production. The 
Province leads in AgriTech innovation and agricultural 
machinery 
manufacturing, 
pioneering 
sustainable 
practices like no-till seeding and embracing Industry 
4.0 through automation and academic collaboration, 
with significant biofuel production capacity, robust 
infrastructure, talent, and financing sources. Córdoba 
presents compelling opportunities across agribusiness 
and technology-driven agriculture. 
Córdoba is home to global manufacturers such as Renault, 
Iveco, Stellantis, and Volkswagen, and more than 250 
local auto parts suppliers, the automotive supply chain of 
the Province produces one-third of the country’s vehicles. 
Stellantis is investing US$385 million to launch new models 
for regional export, while Renault is expanding production 
with the highly anticipated Niagara pickup.
Cluster organizations and partnerships with universities are 
accelerating research in automation, artificial intelligence, 
and alternative energy systems, positioning Córdoba not 
only as a production hub generating + 13,000 jobs, but also 
as a leader in the automotive transformation spreading across 
the Americas.
Connectivity & 
Digital Transformation
Córdoba has more than 3,000 companies, a robust 
and dynamic business ecosystem in AgTech, AI, fintech, 
software, and digital services. Córdoba has more than 
63,000 workers in the sector, representing more than 
11% of total provincial employment, consolidating 
a critical mass of highly specialized professionals. 
Córdoba’s universities play a strategic role in training 
human resources in key areas such as software, 
biotechnology, engineering, and data science.

7
Startup & Innovation 
Ecosystem
Córdoba boasts one of Argentina’s strongest startup 
ecosystems, producing 25% of the country’s new 
ventures. With over 300 startups, 70+ incubators 
and accelerators, and 27,000 annual tech graduates, 
the Province combines talent, creativity, and support 
systems into fertile ground for entrepreneurship. 
The startup ecosystem in Córdoba has experienced 
remarkable 
growth 
between 
2012 
and 
2024, 
where 141 Córdoba-based startups accessed 247 
investment rounds. What makes Córdoba unique is 
the collaborative culture linking universities, industry 
clusters, and international networks, ensuring that its 
startups are not only scaling locally but also reaching 
global markets with innovative, export-ready solutions.
Life Sciences: 
HealthTech & BioTech
Generating nearly US $700 million annually and responsible 
for over 22% of national biotech exports, the Province has 
become a powerhouse in biotechnology, biomedicine, 
and bio-based solutions. Its biotech cluster is fostering 
groundbreaking work in bioplastics, biofuels, biological 
seeds, and agro-biologicals. HealthTech companies are 
developing internationally certified medical technologies 
that meet global demand. Córdoba is open for global 
investors and innovators eager to join a fast-growing sector 
that bridges science, technology, and social impact.

8
 HOST
CITES
CITY OF CÓRDOBA
GENERAL CABRERA
RÍO CUARTO
As Argentina’s second-largest city, Córdoba 
is a vibrant hub of culture, innovation, and 
higher education. Known as the “university 
capital,” it combines a rich industrial tradition 
with a fast-growing technology and startup 
ecosystem, making it a national benchmark for 
competitiveness.
Located in the heart of the agribusiness corridor, 
General Cabrera stands out for its strong food-
processing industry, particularly in peanuts and 
dairy. It is a key driver of Argentina’s agricultural 
exports, linking local production to global 
markets.
Recognized as the “agroindustrial capital,” Río 
Cuarto is a center of advanced agricultural 
production, technology, and research. With 
universities and innovation hubs, it leads 
initiatives in precision agriculture, biotechnology, 
and sustainable food production.
GENERAL DEHEZA
General Deheza is home to major agro-industrial 
companies, including General Deheza Oilseed 
Company, a leader in oilseed processing 
and exports. The city represents Argentina’s 
integration of agricultural resources with large-
scale industrial capacity.

9
ARROYITO
VILLA MARÍA
Known as the birthplace of Arcor, one of the 
world’s largest candy and food companies, 
Arroyito showcases Argentina’s global 
leadership in food processing, exports, and 
sustainable industrial development.
Villa María is Argentina’s dairy capital, leading in 
milk production and processing. Alongside its 
agri-food sector, it has a growing entrepreneurial 
ecosystem and academic institutions that drive 
innovation and local development.
SAN FRANCISCO
San Francisco is a dynamic industrial hub 
with strong clusters in agricultural machinery, 
pharmaceuticals, and logistics. Its modern 
industrial parks and innovation centers position 
it as a leader in regional competitiveness.

10
 ACE CÓRDOBA 
SITES
PALACIO FERREYRA
FERREYRA INDUSTRIAL PARK
PROMEDON
HELACOR S.A. (GRIDO) 
An architectural landmark in the heart of Córdoba, the Palacio 
Ferreyra is a French-style mansion built in the early 20th century and 
home to the Evita Fine Arts Museum. Surrounded by gardens, the 
museum houses an impressive collection of Argentine art from the 
19th century to the present, offering visitors a unique blend of history, 
culture, and artistic expression.
A prominent industrial hub located in the City of Córdoba, Argentina. 
The Park hosts a diverse range of manufacturing operations. Companies 
with facilities in the area include Fiat Chrysler Automobiles (FCA), 
CNH Industrial, Promedon, and Dayco Argentina. These companies 
represent sectors such as automotive manufacturing, agricultural 
machinery production, medical equipment, and automotive parts.
Founded in 1985, Promedon is a leading private company in the 
research, development, production, and commercialization of 
medical technology for surgical procedures. With its headquarters 
in Argentina, Promedon has direct presence and subsidiaries in 
Argentina, Chile, Brazil, Mexico, and Germany. Promedon sells its 
products in over 60 countries
Founded in 2000, Helacor has transformed the ice cream industry 
in Latin America, producing the popular line of Grido ice cream. 
Helacor’s initially produced Grido ice cream based on the belief that 
quality ice cream was too expensive for most Argentinians. Helacor 
grew from a single store into a vast network of over 2,000 franchises. 
It is the largest ice cream chain in the region and the fourth largest 
globally by production volume. Internationally, the company has 
successfully expanded its business model and product offerings to 
Uruguay, Paraguay, Chile, and Peru.

11
CLUB ATLÉTICO TALLERES
PRODEMAN
BIO4
Founded in 1985, Promedon is a leading private company in the 
research, development, production, and commercialization of 
medical technology for surgical procedures. With its headquarters 
in Argentina, Promedon has direct presence and subsidiaries in 
Argentina, Chile, Brazil, Mexico, and Germany. Promedon sells its 
products in over 60 countries
Based in Río Cuarto, the Bio4 specializes in producing bioethanol 
from corn, operating under a bioeconomy model that maximizes 
the use of every grain. The company maintains around 150 
highly skilled direct and indirect jobs, employing engineers, 
biotechnologists, operators, and administrative staff. Its operations 
stimulate the regional economy by sourcing corn from local farmers 
while supporting the livestock industry through DDGS production 
(distillers dried grains with soluble, a high-protein animal feed, and 
corn oil for the food industry), strengthening the entire value chain 
in rural Córdoba.
Club Atlético Talleres Founded in 1913, Club Atlético Talleres is one 
of the leading sports, social, and cultural institutions in Córdoba, 
serving as both an economic driver and a pillar of the province’s 
identity. With infrastructure that includes a modern high-
performance center, the club generates hundreds of direct and 
indirect jobs across multiple sectors.
STELLANTIS 
A multinational automotive manufacturing corporation formed in 
2021 through the merger of Fiat Chrysler Automobiles (FCA) and the 
French PSA Group. The company encompasses 14 automotive brands. 
Its plant in Córdoba, located in Ferreyra, is a cornerstone of vehicle 
production in the region. Stellantis announced an investment of 
US$385 million, aimed at modernizing infrastructure and producing 
a new domestic engine and vehicle family. The company is creating 
more than 1,800 new positions, effectively doubling its current 
workforce. Up to 75% of the new production is expected to be 
exported to regional markets, primarily Brazil.

12
TONADITA
Based in Villa María, Córdoba, Tonadita is a dynamic dairy company 
renowned for its high-quality products and continuous growth 
since its acquisition in 1998. With a modern 15,000 m² plant and a 
dedicated team of 250 employees, the company produces a wide 
range of dairy goods—including butter, cream, spreadable cheeses, 
dulce de leche, and specialty cheeses—serving both South American 
and international markets. Tonadita has expanded steadily over 
the years, opening new plants, launching innovative products, and 
achieving certifications like FSSC 22000 to meet global standards. Its 
exports reach countries across the Americas, Africa, the Middle East, 
and Asia, with a recent successful entry into the U.S. market under 
strict FSMA compliance.
ACEITERA GENERAL DEHEZA (AGD)
AGD is a fully Argentine agro-industrial company headquartered 
in General Deheza, Córdoba. It produces natural foods, vegetable 
oils, protein flours, biodiesel, and refined glycerin. AGD operates 
in eight provinces of Argentina with seven industrial plants, AGD 
executes through four main business units—Agriculture (UNAGRO), 
Brands, Peanuts, and Cereals & Oilseeds (UNCO)—covering everything 
from crop and livestock production to oil refining and consumer 
products, exports to over 45 countries. Renowned for its integration, 
technological diversification, and sustainability practices, AGD has 
over 70 years of history, employs more than 2,700 people, and plays a 
leading role in Argentina’s agro-industrial sector.
RÍO CUARTO NATIONAL UNIVERSITY (UNRC) 
Established in 1971, UNRC is a leading public institution in the 
Province of Córdoba. Its academic community includes over 
1,800 faculty members, nearly 20,000 undergraduate students, 
and around 700 postgraduate students. The university offers 79 
undergraduate and postgraduate programs, structured across five 
faculties: Agronomy and Veterinary Sciences; Economic Sciences; 
Exact, Physical, Chemical and Natural Sciences; Human Sciences; 
and Engineering.

13
SAN FRANCISCO INDUSTRIAL PARK 
The San Francisco Industrial, Technological, and Logistical Park in 
Córdoba is one of Argentina’s largest and most dynamic industrial 
hubs, spanning 250 hectares and hosting over 160 companies 
that generate around 3,000 jobs. Strategically located along 
key bioceanic corridors, it offers state-of-the-art infrastructure 
and services, including advanced utilities, logistics support, and 
business facilities. 
AKRON 
Akron produces self-unloading grain carts, grain baggers and 
extractors, seed and fertilizer wagons, forage compactor boxes, vertical 
feed mixers, and land levelers to efficiently support harvest, post-
harvest, organic fertilization, and forage management tasks. Akron 
has presence in Canada, United States, Australia, Ukraine, Kazakhstan, 
South Africa, France, Uruguay, Paraguay and New Zealand.
COSTANTINI S.A.
An industrial automation company founded in 1993 specializing 
in custom robotic and automated solutions for sectors such as 
automotive, food, and manufacturing. Costantini designs and builds 
tailored systems for welding, cutting, packaging, and palletizing. The 
company serves clients across Argentina and exports to other Latin 
American markets, standing out for its ability to adapt technology to 
each customer’s needs.
INSTITUTE OF DAIRY STUDIES (ESCUELA SUPERIOR 
INTEGRAL DE LECHERÍA - ESIL)
Located in Villa María, ESIL is a leading educational center specializing 
in dairy industry training. It offers technical courses, such as Cheese 
Technology, combining remote theoretical lessons with optional 
practical stages, culminating in formal certification. Many graduates 
work internationally, reflecting its global influence. The school also 
operates a robotic milking facility used for production and guided 
demonstrations. In May 2025, ESIL was appointed by Argentina’s 
Secretariat of Agriculture as technical coordinator for the country’s 
readmission to the International Dairy Federation, reinforcing its role 
on the global stage.

14
CAPITALINAS 
The Capitalinas building complex supports a dynamic business 
ecosystem. It serves as a strategic hub for technology, service, and 
consulting firms, attracting high value investments. Its prime location, 
linking Córdoba’s historic city center with major access routes, 
positions Capitalinas as a gateway for modern business and a symbol 
of the city’s economic diversification and dynamism.
CASA NARANJA X 
GLOBANT 
Naranja X is a leading Argentine fintech firm. Its app provides a full 
financial ecosystem—digital accounts, wallets, investments, loans, 
and virtual cards—serving 1.8 million users, 10 million cards, and 
450,000 businesses. With over 3,000 employees, 170+ branches, 
Naranja X combines strong physical and digital presence while 
promoting access to financial services.
Globant is a multinational company offering technology services, 
consulting, and software development. Founded in 2003, it offers 
a wide range of solutions, including software development, digital 
transformation consulting, AI-driven services, digital twins, mobile 
applications, and immersive experiences. Its innovative AI Pods 
model provides subscription-based AI solutions across industries 
such as entertainment, finance, and energy. With a presence in over 
30 countries and more than 31,100 employees, Globant is a leading 
exporter of knowledge-based services from Latin America, serving 
major clients including Google, Electronic Arts, Nissan, Banco 
Santander, and Formula 1, while generating most of its revenue 
outside Argentina.
ARCOR 
Arcor is a leading Argentine multinational group specializing in food 
products, confectionery, chocolates, cookies, ice creams, agribusiness, 
and packaging. Founded on July 5, 1951, in Arroyito, Córdoba. Arcor 
operates over 40 industrial plants across Latin America, producing 
approximately 3 million kilograms of products daily. It is the world’s 
leading candy manufacturer and one of the top producers of cookies 
and crackers in South America. The company exports its products 
to more than 120 countries and has commercial offices in America, 
Europe, and Asia.
